title: Prismatomeris glabra
intro: Prismatomeris glabra is a plant species belonging to the Rubiaceae family, commonly known as the madder family, and is native to tropical Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Prismatomeris glabra include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Prismatomeris glabra include decoctions and infusions, which are used to treat various health conditions, such as bronchitis and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Prismatomeris glabra is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. This plant is commonly known as smooth prismatomeris. It is native to parts of Asia, including China and India. The classification of Prismatomeris glabra includes its family, genus, and species levels, which are Rubiaceae, Prismatomeris, and P. glabra, respectively. Further classification at the order, class, and kingdom levels would include Gentianales, Magnoliopsida, and Plantae, respectively.
